title: Tauri Desktop Client
problem: The existing egui GUI is no longer the desired long-term desktop product surface. codex-helper needs a polished but simple Tauri client that replaces egui, uses React + Tailwind + shadcn/ui, starts from a component prototype, and preserves safe runtime owner semantics without exposing every advanced control-plane concept at top level.
gates: shadcn/ui prototype reviewed against simplified product UI requirements
cargo check for affected Rust packages
desktop lifecycle action matrix before replacement claims

evidence.summary: Opened workstream and captured replacement intent, React + Tailwind + shadcn/ui stack, component-prototype workflow, and simplified MVP.
Revised direction after user feedback: default UI should be simple and familiar, with advanced sessions/routing/diagnostics progressively disclosed.

Reviewed repo-ref/aio-coding-hub and adopted its Tauri client shell, sidebar runtime footer, page header, dashboard work-status/recent-request panels, providers card/order layout, usage filter/data-panel pattern, and settings two-column pattern while rejecting its broader top-level navigation for the first GUI.

Added UI_BRIEF.md after reviewing the user-provided AIO Coding Hub screenshot, repo-ref/awesome-design-md references, README product capabilities, admin API contracts, and existing egui surfaces. The brief freezes a restrained mint/teal local proxy dashboard direction and includes an imagegen prompt pack.

Accepted Dashboard and simplified credential-list concepts as local image references under docs/workstreams/tauri-desktop-client/assets. Reclassified API keys as provider credentials, removed API Keys as a top-level page in the design brief, and updated UI_BRIEF.md with Providers-as-credentials-owner constraints, pagination/table-density rules, and the next Providers page imagegen prompt.

Accepted the Providers concept as docs/workstreams/tauri-desktop-client/assets/providers-approved-v1.png. The concept validates provider cards with visible auth/env-var fields and a right-side default route order panel. Added the next Usage page imagegen prompt to UI_BRIEF.md.

First Usage concept needs revision. User wants sub2api-style full-width request table, pagination footer, cost tooltip, token direction/icons, and first-token latency, while avoiding a permanent right-side cost panel because costs are only estimates. UI_BRIEF.md Usage prompt was revised accordingly.

Accepted the revised Usage concept as docs/workstreams/tauri-desktop-client/assets/usage-approved-v1.png. The concept keeps a full-width request table, estimated-cost tooltip, token/cache indicators, first-token latency, duration, and practical pagination without a permanent cost panel.

Rejected the first Settings concept as too complex and saved it only as docs/workstreams/tauri-desktop-client/assets/settings-generated-too-complex-v1.png. Added simpler Settings structure references and revised the brief toward responsive two-column settings cards with no permanent right sidebar or giant control-console form.

Generated docs/workstreams/tauri-desktop-client/assets/settings-candidate-v2.png from the simplified two-column Settings prompt. It is pending user review before becoming the accepted Settings baseline.

User accepted the revised Settings concept and it was copied to docs/workstreams/tauri-desktop-client/assets/settings-approved-v1.png. TDC-018 is complete; Dashboard, Providers, Usage, and Settings now have accepted page-level image baselines.

Generated throwaway React 19 + Tailwind CSS 4 + shadcn/ui-style + TanStack prototype under docs/workstreams/tauri-desktop-client/prototype/. It implements Dashboard, Providers, Usage, and Settings with mock data and passes pnpm build.

User accepted the prototype direction with desktop layout feedback. Added IMPLEMENTATION_BRIEF.md with official-source-informed stack choices, fixed app-shell layout rules, and recommended apps/desktop repository structure before production Tauri scaffold work.

User accepted IMPLEMENTATION_BRIEF.md and authorized frontend work. Added apps/desktop React 19 + Tailwind CSS 4 + TanStack production frontend and apps/desktop/src-tauri Tauri v2 crate; imported four-page static/mock UI; pnpm test/build, cargo fmt --check, and cargo check -p codex-helper-desktop pass.

Completed TDC-060 with concerns. Added Tauri get_admin_read_model, frontend admin DTO/client/mapper hooks, Dashboard/Providers/Usage/Settings live read-only data wiring, mock fallback banners, mapper/API tests, and loopback admin API smoke against 127.0.0.1:4211. pnpm test/build, cargo fmt --check, cargo check -p codex-helper-desktop, and git diff --check pass.

Completed TDC-070 with concerns. Added shared runtime data-state taxonomy and read-model hook, upgraded DataStateBanner for loading/unavailable/disconnected/auth-required/empty/stale states, surfaced owner-pending lifecycle uncertainty, and validated with pnpm test/build, cargo fmt --check, cargo check -p codex-helper-desktop, and git diff --check.

Completed TDC-080 with concerns. Added typed Tauri safe mutation commands, admin token propagation, exact confirmation phrases for stop/switch actions, frontend action hooks/status banners, Dashboard/Providers/Settings control wiring, and validation via pnpm build/test, cargo fmt --check, cargo check -p codex-helper-desktop, cargo nextest run -p codex-helper-desktop --lib, and git diff --check.

continue_policy.notes: Next step is TDC-090 tray and authoritative desktop owner semantics. Harden the current CLI-spawned desktop-managed runtime into a first-class tray/sidecar lifecycle path; no normal GUI close/quit may remote-stop an attached runtime.
